dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal implements key reversal logic: (1) uptrend condition approximated as close > sma_20 representing n-day uptrend; (2) current day highest price of n-days mapped to high == dc_upper (Donchian channel upper band over 20 periods); (3) lower low condition as low < prev_low; (4) lower close as close < prev_close. Volatility enhancement condition omitted as 'noticeably higher' lacks numeric threshold and hvol_20 not explicitly referenced. Exit uses reversal to uptrend (close > sma_20). Parameters n and volatility_threshold marked as AUTHOR_SILENT; n approximated as 20-period lookback for Donchian channel and SMA.

Volatility enhancement condition omitted as 'noticeably higher' lacks numeric threshold and hvol_20 not explicitly referenced. Exit uses reversal to uptrend (close > sma_20). Parameters n and volatility_threshold marked as AUTHOR_SILENT; n approximated as 20-period lookback for Donchian channel and SMA.

source_rule_text: Enter a key reversal trade when an uptrend exists over n-days, the current day is the highest price of n-days, a lower low has occurred, and the close is lower, with preference for entries when volatility is noticeably higher than previous days.
